title: US x Iran ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran announced on April 7, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between the United States and Iran, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 7 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect. 

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed two-week period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: **President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely on April 22, 2026, after second-round peace talks in Pakistan were cancelled, allowing time for Tehran's fractured leadership to submit a unified proposal while maintaining the naval blockade of Iranian ports.** This followed an initial two-week truce agreed on April 8 via Pakistani mediation, with in-principle extensions discussed mid-April. On April 27, Iran proposed a new three-stage negotiation framework amid fragile de-escalation, including Strait of Hormuz tensions and recent ship attacks. Upcoming US envoy dispatches to Pakistan for diplomacy represent key catalysts that could shape extension terms, underscoring trader focus on escalation risks versus negotiation progress.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 99.9% implied probability of no change to the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting resilient U.S. economic data that reinforces the Federal Reserve's patient stance amid persistent inflation pressures. March nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs with unemployment steady at 4.3%, weekly jobless claims near lows around 214,000, and March CPI showing energy index up 12.5% year-over-year, supporting the March decision to hold the 3½-3¾% target range. Fed minutes indicated some openness to hikes if inflation reaccelerates from oil shocks. Scenarios challenging this consensus include hotter-than-expected April consumer confidence data today or a hawkish dot plot shift tomorrow, though proximity to resolution limits major repricing.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France's recent 2-1 friendly victory over Brazil on March 26, highlighted by Kylian Mbappé's goal, combined with reclaiming the FIFA #1 ranking on April 1 (1877 points), has elevated trader consensus to a 16.4% implied probability for World Cup glory, edging Spain's 15.4% amid La Roja's Euro 2024 pedigree and Lamine Yamal's emergence. The March 31 playoff finals locked in the full 48-team field without upsets for elites like England (11.2%), Argentina (8.8%), and Brazil (8.6%), but emerging injuries—Hugo Ekitike's Achilles tear for France, Rodrygo's knee issue for Brazil—add caution. Tight clustering underscores parity in an expanded format, favorable group draws, and knockout unpredictability among golden generations.

title: Pistons vs. Magic
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 27 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Orlando Magic hold a 2-1 series lead over the top-seeded Detroit Pistons in their first-round NBA playoffs matchup, fueled by a 113-105 Game 3 home win that avenged Detroit's 98-83 Game 2 rout. Heading into Game 4 at Amway Center, Magic face depth issues with Franz Wagner (ankle), Jonathan Isaac (knee sprain), Anthony Black (abdomen), and Jett Howard (ankle) ruled out per latest injury reports, leaning heavily on Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane. Pistons counter with Kevin Huerter questionable (left hip soreness after Game 3 exit), but Cade Cunningham's playmaking (averaging high usage) and superior 60-22 regular-season record bolster trader consensus on a potential road tie. Home-court advantage and Orlando's defensive grit shape the closely contested implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Timberwolves lead the Nuggets 3-1 in their Western Conference first-round playoff series after a 112-96 Game 4 victory on April 25, powered by Ayo Dosunmu's 43 bench points amid early injuries, but trader consensus has shifted dramatically for Game 5 in Denver, implying 82% probability for a Nuggets win due to Minnesota's depleted roster. Anthony Edwards is out with a hyperextended right knee from Game 4, Donte DiVincenzo sidelined by an Achilles tear, and Rob Dillingham unavailable, forcing reliance on Jaden McDaniels, Julius Randle, and Rudy Gobert against Nikola Jokic's triple-double threat. Denver's Aaron Gordon is questionable (left calf tightness) and Peyton Watson out (hamstring), but home-court advantage and desperation fuel Nuggets' momentum in this must-win to extend the series.

title: When will Bitcoin hit $150k?
description: When will Bitcoin hit $150k

markets.question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by September 30?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' superior 2026 car has propelled teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to the top of trader consensus at 39.5% and 32.5% implied probabilities for the Drivers' Championship, reflecting their podium locks in the opening rounds while rivals like Ferrari's Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton trail in standings and odds. Antonelli leads points by nine after back-to-back wins in China and Japan, showcasing rookie poise with superior average finish (1.33) over Russell's 2.33, but traders favor the veteran Briton's qualifying edge (22-5 head-to-head), racecraft maturity, and history of outdueling top teammates amid 21 races left. Intra-team dynamics intensify ahead of Miami, where rest advantages and tire strategy could shift the tight intra-squad battle, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in this evenly matched duel.

title: Israel x Hezbollah Ceasefire extended by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official extension of the 10-day ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah announced on April 16, 2026, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed extension to the halt in direct military engagement between Israel and Hezbollah, by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Both extensions of the April 16 ceasefire and new agreements scheduled to take effect before or at the initial agreement's scheduled end will be considered extensions of the ceasefire agreement, provided there is no period during which no ceasefire is in effect.

If a qualifying agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire extension ultimately takes effect.

An extension of the ceasefire agreement requires clear public confirmation from both the Israeli government and Hezbollah that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another for longer than the initially agreed 10-day period, or for an official extension of the ceasefire agreement in place to be otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation, or unilateral pause in hostilities without a confirmed agreement on a qualifying extension will not qualify. Similarly, newly agreed-upon humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not qualify.

A newly agreed-upon broader peace deal will qualify if it includes a qualifying extension of the ceasefire agreement/halt in military hostilities. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures, but do not explicitly commit to extending the ceasefire, will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire extension agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following White House talks on April 24, U.S. President Donald Trump announced a three-week extension of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, originally set to expire days earlier, amid fragile compliance with mutual accusations of violations including Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killing at least 14 on April 27 and Hezbollah drone attacks targeting IDF positions. The truce, building on a November 2024 agreement requiring Hezbollah withdrawal north of the Litani River and Israeli pullback, remains strained by Netanyahu's insistence on a reinforced security zone and ongoing border clashes. Traders weigh diplomatic momentum from rare direct Israel-Lebanon negotiations against escalation risks, with the extension expiring mid-May barring further deals or breakdowns.

title: Thunder vs. Suns
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 27 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the Suns win, the market will resolve to "Suns".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder hold a commanding 3-0 series lead over the Phoenix Suns in their Western Conference first-round NBA playoff matchup, with Game 4 set for Phoenix on April 27 amid trader consensus reflecting OKC's dominance. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's playoff career-high 42 points and eight assists propelled a 121-109 Game 3 road win on April 25, following his 37-point effort in Game 2's 120-107 victory, underscoring the Thunder's title-defending momentum from a 64-18 regular season. Jalen Williams remains out with a hamstring strain, thinning OKC's depth, while Suns' Grayson Allen (hamstring) and Jordan Goodwin (calf) are questionable. Phoenix showed fight in Game 3 but faces elimination pressure and home crowd boost as the desperate underdog.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder top trader consensus at 51.5% implied probability to repeat as 2026 NBA champions after a dominant 64-18 regular season that clinched the West's No. 1 seed and home-court advantage through the conference finals, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play anchoring their elite defense and depth despite minor injury concerns. San Antonio Spurs follow at 16.6% as the No. 2 West seed with 62 wins, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's breakout and a 4-1 regular-season edge over OKC, positioning them as the primary Western Conference threat amid ongoing first-round playoff wins like their recent road victory over Portland. Boston Celtics hold 14.6% as Eastern Conference contenders with 56 wins, advancing past Philadelphia in a tight series while navigating the loaded East playoff bracket against teams like Detroit and New York. Odds reflect skin-in-the-game sentiment three days after key road wins by top contenders, with no major injuries altering paths to the NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the narrow frontrunner at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, reflecting bets on his Make America Healthy Again movement's populist appeal and visibility in health policy reforms, despite his public denials of a bid. Vice President J.D. Vance trails closely at 39%, bolstered by recent diplomatic successes including Iran ceasefire negotiations, India-Pakistan peace brokering, and a U.S. nuclear deal in Armenia, alongside incumbency advantages and leads in CPAC straw polls and early surveys from late March 2026. Secretary of State Marco Rubio holds 20% on foreign policy credentials amid Trump administration successes. The race stays tight absent a clear Trump endorsement, with 2026 midterms poised to influence early primary dynamics in Iowa caucuses and New Hampshire.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Governor Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, reflecting recent California primary polls—such as POLITICO's March survey showing him trouncing Kamala Harris 28%-10% and LA Times/UC Berkeley data confirming his edge among state Democrats—as well as his national profile as a vocal Trump administration critic via book tours and media appearances. Progressive Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.5% with strong youth support in April surveys, while Harris lingers at 7.9% amid lingering 2024 campaign baggage. In this wide-open post-incumbent field, consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results in swing states, early fundraising hauls, and endorsements from labor unions or Obama-era figures, with no candidate yet dominating national polling averages.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that Iran's regime will endure past April 30 amid severe economic strain from the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, sanctions, and ongoing 2025–2026 protests fueled by inflation and war escalation. Despite reports of infighting, fleeing officials, and opposition calls from figures like Reza Pahlavi, the leadership remains entrenched, with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) suppressing dissent through internet shutdowns and massacres, as evidenced by recent pro-regime missile parades in Tehran on April 22 and Iran's April 27 offer to reopen the strait in exchange for lifting the blockade. No verifiable mass defections or coups signal imminent overthrow; only extraordinary events like Supreme Leader assassination, IRGC mutiny, or rapid territorial losses could shift odds in the final days.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile Pakistan-mediated ceasefire between the US and Iran, initiated April 8 amid US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and prior airstrikes on Iranian military sites, was extended last week at Islamabad's request to facilitate talks. Yesterday, Iran proposed reopening the strait and ending hostilities while postponing nuclear negotiations, but Tehran rejects direct US talks, insisting on mediation and blaming Washington for stalled diplomacy. Core sticking points—nuclear curbs, sanctions relief, ballistic missiles, and regional security—remain unresolved after marathon Islamabad sessions ended without agreement in mid-April. Traders weigh low near-term odds against potential second-round talks before any truce expiration, with historical US-Iran impasses underscoring uncertainty.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Finland the frontrunner at 36.4% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026, propelled by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin"—a violin-pop fusion that dominated UMK national final on February 28 and has led bookmakers, fan polls, and jury forecasts since song release. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem"), Greece's Akylas ("Ferto" from Sing for Greece), and France's internally selected Monroe ("Regarde!") cluster at 10-11% on strong preview buzz and pre-party acclaim, reflecting Nordic ballad appeal and upbeat entries. With all 35 songs out, OGAE voting and rehearsal footage this week could spark shifts before Vienna semis May 12/14 and final May 16, where jury-televote splits often yield upsets.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 21 - April 28, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 21 12:00 PM ET to April 28, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Elon Musk posting 220-239 times on X from April 21-28, 2026, at a leading 59.5% implied probability, driven by his current pace of 181 posts through 86% of the period per XTracker data (last synced April 27). This reflects a steady daily average of 25-40 posts, fueled by viral engagement on political hot takes—like critiques of Ilhan Omar and Sam Altman—plus Tesla, SpaceX, and cultural commentary spikes, including a 40-post burst in hours on April 26. Recent South Africa-focused threads and election-season buzz have sustained momentum, though activity can swing with breaking news; final tallies hinge on April 28's closing hours to noon ET.

title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim lead in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner at 18.8%, edged by California Governor Gavin Newsom at 17.9%, reflecting early positioning after President Trump's 2024 victory and term limits under the 22nd Amendment. Vance's advantage stems from his March 28 CPAC straw poll win as top GOP successor, recent fundraising with major Republican donors, and a UMass Lowell poll showing him ahead 33%-30% head-to-head. Newsom gains from leading Kamala Harris in Democratic primary surveys and his anti-Trump profile appealing to party base voters. The tight race persists amid 2026 midterm uncertainty—GOP control of Congress could solidify Vance's path-to-victory in swing states, while Democratic gains might elevate Newsom or challengers like Marco Rubio through better polling averages or endorsements.

title: Golden Knights vs. Utah
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 27 at 9:30PM ET:
If the Golden Knights win, the market will resolve to "Golden Knights".
If the Utah win, the market will resolve to "Utah".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Utah Mammoth holds a 2-1 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights in their Western Conference first-round Stanley Cup Playoffs matchup after a 4-2 home win in Game 3 on April 24, where Lawson Crouse scored twice and goaltender Karel Vejmelka stood tall despite Vegas outshooting Utah. Facing a must-win Game 4 on Utah ice at Delta Center on April 27, Golden Knights coach shuffled top-three forward lines—Barbashev-Eichel-Dorofeyev up top—and power-play units to spark a stagnant offense averaging under three goals per game lately. Vegas misses center William Karlsson (lower-body, postseason status uncertain) and winger Jonas Rondbjerg, while Utah sits forward Barrett Hayton (upper body); home-ice advantage, rest edge, and Utah's 5-on-5 scoring (fifth in NHL regular season) fuel trader focus on a potential 3-1 stranglehold versus Vegas' playoff pedigree and road revenge trends.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia Flyers hold a commanding 4-1 series lead over the Pittsburgh Penguins in the Eastern Conference First Round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs after a 5-2 road win in Game 5 at PPG Paints Arena. Dan Vladar has anchored the net with elite goaltending, including a 3-0 shutout in Game 2 despite a right-arm injury scare in Game 3, while Penguins veteran Sidney Crosby fueled their 4-2 Game 4 victory with a goal and assist to stave off elimination. Flyers defenseman Emil Andrae remains out with an upper-body injury, but depth has prevailed in this Battle of Pennsylvania rivalry. Game 6, if necessary on April 29 at Wells Fargo Center, gives PHI home-ice advantage against a desperate PIT squad facing historical 3-11 deficit in similar spots.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and the final trading day during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil front-month futures traded around $96.70 per barrel on April 28, propelled by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, where stalled U.S.-Iran diplomacy and disrupted flows have tightened supply and spiked volatility. Trader sentiment reflects strong consensus on elevated prices, with recent 2% intraday gains underscoring supply security risks over demand worries from softening global refining runs projected to decline 1 million b/d in 2026 per IEA estimates. Modest U.S. crude inventories rose 1.9 million barrels for the week ending April 17, per latest EIA data, while OPEC+ signaled a 206,000 bpd output hike for May amid market strain. Traders eye the April 30 EIA report and potential Middle East escalations as key near-term catalysts before monthly resolution.

title: Trump Today: April 25
description: This is a market over what Donald Trump will do on April 25, 2026.

markets.question: Will Trump dance today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump dances between 12:00AM ET and 11:59PM ET on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Dancing" is defined as deliberate, rhythmic body movement typically matched music or a beat, such as swaying, stepping, or coordinated hand or body motions. Casual gesturing, clapping, or incidental body movement will not qualify. Dancing without the presence of music or a beat will qualify.

AI-generated content, deepfakes, or altered footage will not be considered. 

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

This market will resolve based on video.

markets.question: Will Trump wear a red tie today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump wears a red tie at any point on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The color will be determined by the most visually dominant color visible in publicly available photos or videos. For example, for the tie to be considered predominantly blue, its primary color must be a shade of blue, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. This tie would count as "Blue": https://i.ytimg.com/vi/20w7J2JAmqw/maxresdefault.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "No". https://www.usatoday.com/gcdn/authoring/authoring-images/2025/01/20/USAT/77837111007-xxx-dcmg-105-5190802.jpg

If Donald Trump does not wear a tie or if no photos or video are released from the listed date, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump talk to Xi Jinping today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual talks with Donald Trump on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

A talk is defined as any interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Trump sign an executive order today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve "Yes" if Donald Trump signs an executive order on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Executive actions will not qualify toward this market’s resolution.

This market will immediately resolve "Yes" if the text of an executive order for the given day is published on the White House page for presidential actions (https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/) or the White House press pool. Mere announcements will not qualify.

If no executive order is published by 12:00 PM ET the day after the listed date of this market, this market will resolve to “No”.

In the case of ambiguity this market may remain open until it is confirmed by the federal register.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Sleepy Joe" today?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term on April 25, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner on April 25 disrupted President Trump's attendance, with gunshots fired near security leading to his evacuation alongside the First Lady and Vice President JD Vance; suspect Cole Thomas Allen was arrested unarmed, and no senior officials were injured. Trump delivered public remarks confirming safety and shared surveillance footage on Truth Social later that evening. No executive order was published on the White House presidential actions page. Trader consensus reflects uncertainty over verifiable video evidence for daily activities like dancing or attire, pending UMA oracle review for market resolution amid high trading volume and evidentiary disputes. Ongoing Iran Strait tensions remain secondary context.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran peace negotiations remain stalled after direct high-level talks in Islamabad around April 12 ended without agreement, prompting President Trump to cancel planned envoy trips to Pakistan two days ago, citing strong US leverage amid the ongoing conflict. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed Washington for the impasse yesterday before meeting Russian President Putin, who praised Iran's "heroic" resistance, signaling Tehran's pivot toward regional alliances like Russia. Core disputes center on Iran's nuclear program, Strait of Hormuz access, Hezbollah activities, and sanctions relief. Pakistani mediators persist, with an Army Chief visit to Tehran potentially key, though no second-round date is set before May deadlines.

title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. FC Bayern München
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, April 28, 2026 between Paris Saint-Germain FC and FC Bayern München.

markets.question: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-28?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 28, 2026
If Paris Saint-Germain FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg at Parc des Princes, trader consensus reflects a razor-thin edge for Paris Saint-Germain at 40.5% implied probability, fueled by their near-fully fit squad—only Quentin Ndjantou sidelined with a hamstring issue—and a timely return confirmed by Luis Enrique, bolstering midfield options like Vitinha amid strong recent knockout form. FC Bayern München trails closely at 34.5% despite their Bundesliga crown and five straight Champions League wins over PSG historically, hampered by key absences including Serge Gnabry (season-ending thigh), Raphaël Guerreiro (muscle tear), and doubts over Tim Bischof and Lennart Karl, plus coach Vincent Kompany's suspension forcing assistant oversight. The 24.5% draw price underscores the high-stakes tactical battle and potential for a cautious opener between these attacking powerhouses.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal handling over 90% of its crude shipments in the Persian Gulf, remains firmly under Tehran’s control despite repeated US airstrikes on military targets, most recently on April 7, 2026, which spared oil infrastructure. Iranian state media has released footage affirming complete security and uninterrupted operations amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict, even as storage tanks near capacity from export disruptions via the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump’s March threats of ground seizure or blockade to pressure reopening the strait have not advanced to invasion, cooling trader speculation after mid-April. Upcoming diplomatic talks or escalation signals, including potential Iranian retaliation against Gulf energy sites, could shift dynamics before any resolution deadlines.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 28?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: New York Yankees vs. Texas Rangers
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the New York Yankees and Texas Rangers, scheduled for April 27 at 8:05PM ET:

This market will resolve to "New York Yankees" if the New York Yankees win the game.

This market will resolve to "Texas Rangers" if the Texas Rangers win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Yankees enter their April 28 matchup at Globe Life Field with a strong 18-10 record atop the AL East, riding solid momentum from recent road games, while the Texas Rangers sit at 14-14 in the AL West amid inconsistent home form at 6-6. A marquee pitching duel anchors trader focus: Yankees' Cam Schlittler (3-1, 1.77 ERA) faces Rangers ace Jacob deGrom (2-0, 2.13 ERA), both delivering early-season dominance that could suppress scoring in the hitter-friendly park. Giancarlo Stanton's right calf tightness looms large after a Monday MRI, potentially landing him on the 10-day injured list and thinning the Yankees' power lineup; Rangers miss Wyatt Langford (forearm IL). Yankees hold a 6-4 edge in the last 10 head-to-heads, but deGrom's return bolsters Texas rest advantage post-series opener.

title: Trump out as President by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump resigns or is removed as President or otherwise ceases to be the President of the United States for any period of time by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Donald Trump's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify. Temporary removal (e.g. temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment under Section 3 or a Section 4 invocation not sustained by both Houses of Congress) or impeachment without removal will not count.

A sustained invocation of the Twenty-Fifth Amendment, Section 4 (i.e., if both Houses of Congress, by two-thirds vote, uphold the Vice President and Cabinet’s determination of presidential inability) will qualify for a "Yes" resolution. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 99.9% reflecting the formidable constitutional barriers to presidential removal, including the need for a House impeachment majority and two-thirds Senate conviction amid Republican control of both chambers, alongside no invocation of the 25th Amendment or indications of resignation. Recent Democratic rhetoric for ousting Trump following early April threats against Iran subsided after he agreed to a two-week ceasefire, while White House statements quashed unfounded hospitalization rumors on April 4. Controversies like Trump's response to Robert Mueller's March 29 death drew criticism but prompted no formal proceedings. Realistic shifts could stem from a sudden health crisis at age 79, GOP defections enabling impeachment, or unforeseen escalation in foreign policy.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certain 99.7% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic normalizing by April's end, driven by entrenched US-Iran dual blockades amid escalating conflict. Real-time vessel tracking data shows daily transits at 2-5 ships—versus pre-war norms of ~140—limited to Iran-aligned tankers, with recent US seizures and Iranian closures halting broader commercial flows. Oil benchmarks have surged on supply fears, while insurance premiums spike and thousands of seafarers remain stranded. Tail risks include a surprise diplomatic breakthrough or unilateral US stand-down before April 30 resolution, though recent failed ceasefires and April 27 turnbacks underscore persistent deadlock.

title: Which company has the best AI model end of April?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an overwhelming 98.1% implied probability to Anthropic holding the top spot for the best AI model by April's end, driven primarily by its April 16 release of Claude Opus 4.7, which surged to the #1 position on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard—the market's official resolution source—with superior Elo scores in coding, reasoning, and overall performance. This follows the earlier gated preview of the even more capable Claude Mythos on April 7, widening Anthropic's lead over rivals like OpenAI's GPT-5.x and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro. With resolution imminent on April 30, the strong positioning reflects real capital betting on sustained dominance amid no recent challengers; realistic threats include a surprise last-minute model drop from competitors topping the leaderboard, though timelines and benchmark gaps make this improbable.
